headline: 3 Reasons to Avoid LMT and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Even during a down period for the markets, Lockheed Martin has gone against the grain, climbing to $619.00. Its shares have yielded a 24% return over the last six months, beating the S&P 500 by 26.8%. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the run-up might have investors contemplating their next move.

headline: Redwire Cameras And Sensors Put At Center Of Artemis II Story
summary: Redwire (NYSE:RDW) will supply its optical imaging system, navigation cameras, and solar sensor technology for NASA’s Artemis II crewed mission on the Orion spacecraft. The company is producing and testing the Orion Camera System and redundant Coarse Sun Sensors under contracts with Lockheed Martin and Airbus for Artemis missions I to V. This hardware will support core navigation, inspection, and livestream video for what is planned as the first crewed flight in NASA’s Artemis program. For...

headline: Lockheed Martin-built Orion Spacecraft Sends Astronauts Moonward for the First Time in 53 Years
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft – built by Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) – launched on the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket from Kennedy Space Center, Florida at 6:35 p.m. ET today, marking the start of NASA's historic Artemis II mission and a crewed return to the Moon.

headline: Why Is Redwire (RDW) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
summary: Shares of aerospace and defense company Redwire (NYSE:RDW) jumped 10.1% in the afternoon session after the company announced its advanced optical imaging and sun sensor technology will be included on NASA's Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight of the Artemis program.
